title: Chengiodendron minor
intro: Chengiodendron minor, also known as the Minor Chengiodendron tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae, native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are beneficial in treating various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Chengiodendron minor include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared into decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
